---
title: "ECON 105: Statistics for Economists"

institution: "UCLA"
term: "Fall 2025"
role: "Instructor"
order: 1

description: >
  Undergraduate statistics course covering probability, random variables,
  estimation, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, and applied data analysis.

syllabus: "../../files/teaching/econ105-syllabus.pdf"
evaluations: "../../files/teaching/econ105-evaluations.pdf"
---

## Course overview

This course introduces probability and statistical methods for economists, with an emphasis on intuition, applications, and data analysis.

## Topics

- Probability and conditional probability
- Discrete and continuous random variables
- Expected value and variance
- Binomial, Poisson, uniform, and normal distributions
- Central limit theorem
- Confidence intervals
- Hypothesis testing
- Introductory data analysis in Excel and R

## Materials

- [Syllabus](../../files/teaching/econ105_syllabus_fall2025_updated.pdf)
- [Lecture slides](#)
- [Practice problems](#)
- [Data exercises](#)
